story: At a minimum, HDTV has twice the linear resolution of standard-definition television (SDTV), thus showing greater detail than either analog television or regular DVD. The technical standards for broadcasting HDTV also handle the 16:9 aspect ratio images without using letterboxing or anamorphic stretching, thus increasing the effective image resolution.
q1: HDTV broadcast standards handle 16:9 aspect ratio images by applying letterboxing to preserve image resolution. (True/False?)
